core_identity.title: Pythagoras' Theorem: Right Triangles and Hidden Squares
core_identity.author: Gerry Docherty

curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Use and prove Pythagoras’ Theorem (a² + b² = c²) to find unknown lengths in right-angled triangles and justify solutions in applied design contexts.

verse_ality.driving_question: How can Pythagoras’ Theorem help us prove a relationship in right triangles and uncover hidden lengths in the world around us?

knowing_world.focus: Explore the area relationship in a right-angled triangle, form the equation a² + b² = c², identify the hypotenuse, and practise solving for missing sides (including challenges).
key_concepts: Rearranging to find an unknown side
tools: Video: Building the formula a² + b² = c² (canva_embed): https://www.canva.com/design/DAGrWhKDHX0/yY02fHT4G4s3gv2nyAUVhg/watch?embed
Interactive: Label a, b and c on right triangles (canva_embed): https://www.canva.com/design/DAGsHAeyaq0/Pld8UYIYrLIaOV7ctWn21w/view?embed
Video: Application to find the height of an isosceles triangle (canva_embed): https://www.canva.com/design/DAGrW0FYw3g/RT8XMP2G4OQp2FUt4lwNzQ/watch?embed

human_story.focus: Place the theorem in its historical journey (Babylonian/Egyptian evidence, Greek proof culture) and explore related ideas like Pythagorean triples, dissection proofs, and irrationality (√2).
justice_prompts: Was Hippasus treated justly? What does his story make you think about mathematics?

expressive_self.focus: Explain Pythagoras’ Theorem in your own voice and represent it visually, noticing how confidence and emotion shift as understanding grows.
activities: Explain what Pythagoras’ Theorem means (choose: voice note, short paragraph, or a poem such as a haiku).
Sketch a right triangle, label a, b, and c, and show the squares on each side with arrows/colour to represent the relationship.
Check in: How do you feel using this idea (curious, confused, clear)?

designed_world.focus: Use Pythagoras’ Theorem to solve design problems where lengths must be accurate and inclusive, and test understanding by creating and checking triangles with peers.
design_challenges: Playground design: a 3 m vertical post and 4 m base—find the diagonal rope length.
Buddy challenge: draw a right triangle, swap with a classmate, and calculate the missing side; verify results together.
Accessible ramp: building height 90 cm, minimum slope ratio 1:12—find the shortest horizontal distance and ramp length; reflect on inclusive design.

regulation_supports: Pause: slow breath and notice how you feel right now.
Move: stretch your arms into a triangle shape and notice the angles.
Breathe: trace a right triangle in the air—inhale 3, hold 2, exhale 4.

pathways.name: Proof Submission - Photo
pathways.focus: Create the Tilted Square Proof of Pythagoras’ Theorem using the given diagram, showing clear reasoning and correct calculations.
products: Photo of your diagram and working out
Self-assessment note on confidence and next steps

pathways.name: Proof Submission - Written Explanation
pathways.focus: Write a structured proof using the given diagram information, explaining each step and calculation clearly.
products: Self-assessment note on confidence and next steps

pathways.name: Proof Submission - Voice/Video Walkthrough
pathways.focus: Talk through your proof of the theorem using the tilted square diagram, explaining decisions and checking accuracy.
products: Self-assessment note on confidence and next steps

required: Identifying right angles and triangle parts
